states.name: Endless Rizette
states.title: Maid of Midwinter
states.description:
Rizette always enjoyed helping out around the house. As trivial as that memory seems now, she holds it close to her heart. There was a time she couldn't remember anything of herself. Not her past, not her friends, nothing beyond Orcanix's latest orders. Sometimes, she's afraid she'll slip back into that horrific void.

So she remembers how it felt to tidy up her family's meager home. How her heart raced as she nursed Finn back to health. Perhaps she wasn't heroic before her fall, but she refuses to think those little acts of kindness meant nothing.

Keeping her loved ones safe and happy was the only life she ever wanted.

states.name: Endless Rizette
states.title: Maid of Midwinter
states.description:
She didn't love Finn as soon as she saw him. That would be too much like a fairy tale. When he arrived beaten and broken from the attack on his village, Rizette nursed him the same way she would any new refugee. They called her the Angel of the Outer City precisely because she never turned anyone away.

Still, she couldn't deny the kinship between them, both teenagers ripped from their families by the undead. When he had no place to go, it was only natural she invite him to live with her and her brother. How could she have known where it would lead?

How could she have known their growing love would lead to ruin?

states.name: Endless Rizette
states.title: Maid of Midwinter
states.description:
They went to fight Orcanix on the darkest night of the year, the bitter midwinter solstice prophesied by Callen and the Sages. Despite the biting cold, Rizette had always loved the holiday. It brought everyone she loved together, snug and cozy around a blazing fire. She thought she'd love it even more when it became the day they freed their people.

Even now, after everything she went through, she can't bring herself to hate wintertime. When the snow falls soft on the ground and the world stands still, she doesn't think of the night she lost everything. She doesn't think of the war, or the long path ahead of them towards peace.

She closes her eyes, remembers that all is well, and reaches for the love that keeps her warm.
